codeReview.summary: The fix commit directly resolves the prior blocking failure: Slack authorization is now fail-closed when `authorizedSlackUserIds` is empty/undefined, and unauthorized users are rejected before injection. The regression test suite was updated to cover empty-list/undefined fail-closed expectations and preserves unauthorized-user checks.

issues.description: ReplyConfig comment still says `authorizedSlackUserIds` empty means all channel users allowed, which is now inconsistent with runtime fail-closed behavior and can mislead future changes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission feature metadata references `skillName: bugfix-worker`, but the expected skill file is not present; the worker transcript shows a documented deviation because the skill could not be invoked.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json (`fix-slack-auth-fail-closed`) sets `skillName` to `bugfix-worker`; `/home/paul/oh-my-claudecode/.factory/skills/bugfix-worker/SKILL.md` is missing (ENOENT); handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false with this exact reason.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The expected shared service registry file is absent, reducing reproducibility of service-related review context.
The expected shared architecture/library directory is absent, limiting standardized architecture-reference checks during scrutiny.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Listing `/home/paul/oh-my-claudecode/.factory/library` failed because the directory does not exist.

summary: PASS: this fix adequately addresses the prior `fix-security-bugs` blocking issue by making Slack authorization fail-closed when no authorized users are configured. No blocking issues found in the fix diff.
